name: Urdabrunnr
description: Urdabrunnr, meaning "Well of Urd" or "Well of Fate," is one of the three most important wells located at the roots of Yggdrasil, the World Tree, in Norse mythology. It is situated in Asgard, the realm of the gods, near the hall of the Norns. The well is a source of wisdom and fate, and it is believed to be where the Norns, the female beings who shape destiny, reside. They draw water from Urdabrunnr to nourish Yggdrasil, ensuring its continued life and the stability of the cosmos. The water is so sacred that anything it touches becomes pure white. Urdabrunnr is also associated with the past, as its name implies a connection to what has already come to pass. The Norns, specifically Urd (fate), Verdandi (present), and Skuld (future), use the well's waters to write the fates of men and gods upon wooden tablets, determining the course of events in the universe. The well is a vital source of knowledge and power, influencing the very fabric of reality in Norse cosmology. It symbolizes the interconnectedness of past, present, and future and the inescapable nature of destiny. Offerings are sometimes left at the well to appease the Norns or seek their favor. The well's continuous flow represents the constant unfolding of time and the ever-changing nature of existence. Its proximity to the Norns underscores their role as the guardians and arbiters of fate, making Urdabrunnr a place of immense significance in the Norse worldview.
subtitle: Well of Fate at Yggdrasil's Root

keyMyths.title: Nourishing Yggdrasil
keyMyths.description: The Norns draw water from Urdabrunnr to water Yggdrasil, preventing it from rotting and ensuring the tree, and thus the cosmos, continues to thrive. This process highlights the Norns' vital role in maintaining cosmic order.

keyMyths.title: Writing of Fate
keyMyths.description: The Norns carve runes into a wooden tablet using water from Urdabrunnr to determine the fate of every being, from gods to mortals. This act emphasizes the Norns' power over destiny and the unchangeable nature of fate in Norse belief.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, describing Urdabrunnr and the Norns' role.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology, describing Urdabrunnr and the Norns' role.

sources.author: Various
sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems that reference Urdabrunnr and its significance.
sources.text: A collection of Old Norse poems that reference Urdabrunnr and its significance.
